title: The Frontier AI Price War Settles at 100 Dollars a Month, and Gemini 3.5 Pro Lands This Month
desc: Google AI Ultra, ChatGPT Pro and Claude Max now all sit at 100 dollars a month, Gemini CLI sunsets June 18, and Gemini 3.5 Pro ships this month. What to budget.
keywords: ai coding subscription, google ai ultra, gemini 3.5 pro, claude max, chatgpt pro, ai tooling cost, gemini cli sunset, antigravity
cta: Want an AI build without wasting subscription spend?
body:
June 12, 2026. The price of building with frontier AI has quietly standardized. Google, OpenAI and Anthropic now each anchor a premium tier at one hundred dollars a month, and this is the month Google is expected to ship Gemini 3.5 Pro to everyone. If your business runs on AI tools, or you pay an agency that does, the cost and the cutoffs just became easier to plan around.

What happened

  1. One hundred dollars a month is the new premium tier. Google's AI Ultra plan sits at that price for developers and power users, with roughly five times the usage of the Pro plan, priority access to its Antigravity coding platform and 20TB of storage. OpenAI's ChatGPT Pro and Anthropic's Claude Max are priced the same, so the three leading labs now line up at the same number.
  2. Gemini 3.5 Pro lands this month. Unveiled at Google I/O on May 19 with a two million token context window and a Deep Think reasoning mode, it was promised for general availability the following month. On stage, Sundar Pichai asked the audience to give Google until next month to ship it, which puts the window squarely in June.
  3. The consumer Gemini CLI sunsets June 18. Google is retiring the Gemini CLI and Code Assist extensions for individual AI Pro, AI Ultra and free users on June 18, 2026, and pushing them to the new Antigravity CLI. Teams on Standard or Enterprise licenses keep uninterrupted access.
  4. Agentic coding is the real battleground. Google's Antigravity 2.0 is an agent first development platform meant to take an idea to a working app, and the one hundred dollar tiers are largely a fight over who hosts the agents that write your software.

What it means for operators

For an agency or a small team, AI tooling is now a real budget line, not a rounding error, and it pays to choose deliberately. Pick the tier that matches your actual workflow rather than the loudest benchmark, and expect to run more than one provider. If your team uses the Gemini CLI, plan the move to Antigravity before June 18 so nothing breaks mid project. Most important, build so you are not locked to a single lab's pricing. The AI automation systems we build are model agnostic, so we can route work to whichever model is best and cheapest for the task, and swap when prices or quality shift. If you would rather hire the skill than manage the subscriptions, our AI engineers do this daily.

The headline number is the same across all three labs now. The advantage goes to whoever uses the tools well, not whoever pays the most.
faqs: How much do the top AI plans cost in 2026?

Google AI Ultra, OpenAI ChatGPT Pro and Anthropic Claude Max each have a premium tier at about one hundred dollars a month. Each bundles higher usage limits and priority access to the lab's coding and agent tools.
When is Gemini 3.5 Pro coming out?

Google unveiled it at I/O on May 19, 2026 and targeted general availability the following month, so the expected window is June 2026. It features a two million token context window and a Deep Think reasoning mode.
What happens to Gemini CLI on June 18, 2026?

Google is retiring the Gemini CLI and Code Assist extensions for individual Pro, Ultra and free users on that date and moving them to the Antigravity CLI. Standard and Enterprise license holders keep access.
Should my business standardize on one AI provider?

Usually not. Prices and model quality shift constantly, so a model agnostic setup that can route work to the best tool for each task protects you from lock in and lets you adopt new releases without rebuilding.
